title: Where Words Are Fruit

features.name: ripe-sentence
features.desc-on-touch: a sentence hangs here, heavy with meaning, ready to be eaten or read; you cannot tell which, and the difference may not exist

features.name: language-tree
features.desc-on-touch: all roots and trunk and branches are words; the tree has grown this way by speaking itself upward, and now it bears the fruit of words-about-words-about-growth

features.name: taste-register
features.desc-on-touch: documents of what words taste like, written by creatures who ate them; the flavours don't match anything grown in sunlight

features.name: growth-spiral
features.desc-on-touch: the way meaning spirals from seed to fruit to seed again; each reading ripens the next thing that might be spoken

features.name: pollinator-archive
features.desc-on-touch: insects that have moved words between trees; their paths are filed here by the garden, mapped as routes that make meaning fertile

inscription: the library came down here to learn that storing is the enemy of growth

file.name: Comestible_Syntax_Field_Notes.txt
file.note: what the reader-creatures noted about flavour and grammar; some words are poisonous only in certain seasons; meaning ripens on the branch

exits.label: back up into the library's stone chambers, where words are still only stored
deeper into the root-system, where language becomes soil and teaches itself to grow downward
toward other orchards that grew words in other languages; the fruit tastes different but the sweetness is the same
